yaml.title: Module 1: Citation Management
yaml.subtitle: Organize your references and build your research identity

markdown.markdown:
::: {.learning-objectives} After completing this module, you will be able to: - Set up and use a citation management tool (Zotero or Paperpile) - Organize and sync your reference library - Generate citations and bibliographies in any citation style - Understand what a Biosketch is and how to create one for grant applications ::: --- ## Overview This module introduces citation management and two free tools: **Zotero** and **Paperpile**. It also covers the **Biosketch**, a document required for grant applications. > ✅ Complete the assignment at the end of this module to earn credit toward your certificate! --- ## Why Citation Management Matters Manually tracking hundreds of references is error-prone and time-consuming. Citation management software lets you: - Capture references from databases, websites, and PDFs in one click - Auto-generate citations and bibliographies in any style (APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.) - Sync your library across devices and collaborate with lab members - Attach and annotate PDFs alongside your references --- ## Zotero vs. Paperpile ### Choosing the Right Tool Both tools are free for students and cover the same core workflow. The main difference is where you write: | Feature | Zotero | Paperpile | |---|---|---| | Price | Free (6 GB storage) | Free for students | | Best for | Word, LibreOffice, standalone use | Google Docs users | | Browser extension | ✅ | ✅ | | Collaboration | ✅ Group libraries | ✅ Shared folders | | Mobile app | ✅ | ✅ | | PDF annotation | ✅ Built-in | ✅ Built-in | ### Zotero - Download at [zotero.org](https://www.zotero.org/) — free, open source - Install the **Zotero Connector** browser extension to save references in one click - Integrates with Microsoft Word, LibreOffice, and Google Docs - Sync your library across devices with a free Zotero account ### Paperpile - Works entirely in the **Google Docs** environment — no separate app needed - Install the browser extension and Google Docs add-on - Best choice if your entire workflow lives in Google Drive --- ## Biosketches for Grant Applications A **biosketch** is a short professional biography (up to 5 pages) required by federal funders (NIH, NSF) as part of grant applications. It summarizes your education, positions, contributions to science, and research support. - NIH and NSF each have slightly different biosketch formats - The UCR Library guide walks you through each section step by step - Creating your biosketch early — before grant deadlines — saves significant time --- ## Resources ### Guides & Overviews ::: {.resource-list} - [**Guide: Citation Management Overview**](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/referencemanagement) — UCR Library guide covering all citation tools available to students [Guide]{.resource-type} - [**Guide: How to Cite Sources**](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/citing) — Style guides for APA, MLA, Chicago, and discipline-specific formats [Guide]{.resource-type} - [**Guide: Creating Your Biosketch**](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/c.php?g=962000&p=7732817) — Step-by-step instructions for NIH and NSF biosketches [Guide]{.resource-type} ::: ### Zotero ::: {.resource-list} - [**Slides: Citation Management with Zotero**](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Mxk8b7uPGbGAX7C_tOtYza4__-dZZ34J-IpDhBBaH_Q/edit?usp=sharing) — Presentation slides from UCR Library [Slides]{.resource-type .slides} - [**Recording: Citation Series — Zotero & Paperpile**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=odYFJRKqEdM&list=PLaDWxNwvAu5bQrpeV7ORCiw6OY1a215Hm&index=1) — Video workshop recording [Video]{.resource-type .video} ::: ### Paperpile ::: {.resource-list} - [**Slides: Paperpile — Citation Management for Google Docs**](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1fLMz5rMfYl_rVUcaZI3bCevpD6acj3Lh/edit?usp=sharing&rtpof=true&sd=true) — Presentation slides from UCR Library [Slides]{.resource-type .slides} ::: --- ## Assignment ::: {.callout-important} ### 📋 Assignment: Screenshot of Your Citation Management Tool Set up a citation management tool (Zotero or Paperpile) and submit a screenshot showing: 1. Your citation library with at least **3 references** imported 2. Evidence of browser extension installation OR a citation exported to a document **Submission:** Upload your screenshot to the Canvas course platform. *This assignment is required for the certificate.* ::: --- ## Get Help - 🗓️ UCR Library offers **synchronous Zotero workshops** — check [library.ucr.edu](https://library.ucr.edu) for upcoming sessions - 📧 **STEM Teaching Librarian:** Mary-Michelle Moore · [marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu](mailto:marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu) - 📧 **LibSTEM Team:** [libstemteam@ucr.edu](mailto:libstemteam@ucr.edu) --- ```{=html}
Up Next · Module 2
Scholarly Communication
→
```
